title: Mercury Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Mercury** is a US business-banking platform exposing a REST API under `https://api.mercury.com/api/v1`. This connector ‚ÄĒ `MercuryConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "mercury"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) ‚ÄĒ wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Mercury organization:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Accounts | `/accounts` + `/account/{id}` | List + read accounts under the organization |
| Transactions | `/account/{id}/transactions` | Paginated, date / status / search-filterable ledger |
| Recipients | `/recipients` + `/recipient/{id}` | List + read + create payment recipients |
| Money Movement | `POST /account/{id}/transactions` | ACH / wire / check sends (Idempotency-Key mandatory) |
| Treasury Statements | `/account/{id}/statements` | Monthly statement records for a date range |

The connector normalises accounts + transactions + recipients + statements into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC ‚ÄĒ all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::MercuryHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Mint a Mercury API token

1. Sign in to https://app.mercury.com.
2. Open **Settings ‚Üí API Tokens** (top-right account menu).
3. Click **Generate API Token**. Choose **Read** or **Read & Write** scope ‚ÄĒ use Read & Write if you need to initiate ACH/wire transfers via `send_payment()`.
4. Copy the token value ‚ÄĒ Mercury shows it only once.
5. (Optional) From **Dashboard ‚Üí Accounts**, copy the **Account ID** of the account you want as the per-tenant default.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP ‚Üí Manage Connectors ‚Üí Mercury**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_token` (secret) | Token from step 4 |
| `default_account_id` (text, optional) | Default account UUID ‚ÄĒ methods accept account_id override per call |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.mercury.com/api/v1` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `api_token` is non-empty, persists config, then probes `GET /accounts` so a bad token fails install loud (returns `AuthStatus.TOKEN_EXPIRED` on 401/403).

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists accounts as a lightweight probe. On 2xx ‚Üí `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 ‚Üí `OFFLINE + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 ‚Üí `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 ‚Üí `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Mercury REST API uses **server-to-server API token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry (tokens are valid until rotated).

### Credential model

- `api_token` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <api_token>`.
- Mercury requires an **`Idempotency-Key`** header on every money-movement POST. The connector enforces a non-empty key in `send_payment()`; use `MercuryConnector.new_idempotency_key()` to mint one per logical attempt.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_token`. Initialises `MercuryHTTPClient`. Probes `GET /accounts` so a bad token fails install loud. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a TokenInfo wrapping the static token ‚ÄĒ no exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /accounts` ‚ÄĒ minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called ‚ÄĒ no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None ‚ÄĒ `api_token` lives in `self.config`. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_token>
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json
Idempotency-Key: <uuid>          ‚Üź only on send_payment()
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` ‚ÄĒ never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_token` | secret | yes | `self.api_token` | Bearer token value |
| `default_account_id` | text | no | `self.default_account_id` | Per-call default account_id for sync() + money-movement |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.mercury.com/api/v1`) | Override for Mercury sandbox or proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_MERCURY_BASE = "https://api.mercury.com/api/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `MercuryConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::MercuryHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_token`; initialise `MercuryHTTPClient`; probe `GET /accounts`. Maps 401/403/429 via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth ‚ÄĒ returns a TokenInfo wrapping the static token.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists accounts via `GET /accounts` as a lightweight probe. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Mercury accounts + transactions ‚Üí `NormalizedDocument` ‚Üí `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Accounts
children.content:
### `async list_accounts() -> Dict`
`GET /accounts` ‚ÄĒ every account on the organization.

### `async get_account(account_id: str) -> Dict`
`GET /account/{account_id}` ‚ÄĒ full record (balances, routing, status, type).

children.title: Transactions
children.content:
### `async list_account_transactions(account_id, *, limit=50, offset=0, status=None, start=None, end=None, order='desc', search=None) -> Dict`
`GET /account/{account_id}/transactions` ‚ÄĒ paginated, filterable ledger. `status` ‚ąą pending / sent / failed / cancelled; `start`/`end` are ISO 8601 date strings; `order` ‚ąą asc / desc; `search` is a free-text match against counterparty / memo.

### `async get_transaction(account_id, transaction_id) -> Dict`
`GET /account/{account_id}/transaction/{transaction_id}` ‚ÄĒ fetch one transaction.

children.title: Recipients
children.content:
### `async list_recipients() -> Dict`
`GET /recipients` ‚ÄĒ list every payment recipient on the org.

### `async get_recipient(recipient_id) -> Dict`
`GET /recipient/{recipient_id}`.

### `async create_recipient(name, emails=None, default_payment_method=None, payment_methods=None) -> Dict`
`POST /recipient` ‚ÄĒ create a new payment recipient. `payment_methods` is a list of Mercury PaymentMethod objects (ACH, wire, check) ‚ÄĒ see Mercury docs for the per-method shape.

children.title: Money Movement
children.content:
### `async send_payment(account_id, recipient_id, amount, payment_method, idempotency_key, note=None, external_memo=None) -> Dict`
`POST /account/{account_id}/transactions` ‚ÄĒ initiate an ACH / wire / check send.

Mercury requires an **`Idempotency-Key`** header on every money-movement POST. The connector raises `MercuryError` if `idempotency_key` is empty. Use the static helper `MercuryConnector.new_idempotency_key(prefix='shielva-mercury')` to mint a UUID-backed key per logical attempt ‚ÄĒ retries reuse the same key so Mercury de-dupes.

children.title: Treasury Statements
children.content:
### `async list_statements(account_id, start, end) -> Dict`
`GET /account/{account_id}/statements?start={ISO}&end={ISO}` ‚ÄĒ fetch treasury statement records for a date range. `start` and `end` are ISO 8601 date strings (`YYYY-MM-DD`).

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
MercuryError                          # base; carries status_code + response_body
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ MercuryAuthError                  # 401 / 403
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ MercuryBadRequestError            # 400
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ MercuryNotFoundError              # 404
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ MercuryConflictError              # 409
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ MercuryRateLimitError             # 429 ‚ÄĒ retry_after_s
‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ MercuryServerError                # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
MercuryNetworkError = MercuryServerError    # legacy name from older code
MercuryNotFound     = MercuryNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `0.5 * 2 ** attempt` ¬Ī 25% jitter, retry up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.TransportError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `MercuryNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429 ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)   ‚ÄĒ logged as warning
net ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,  CONNECTED)
other ‚Üí ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_token` blank | Re-enter the token in ACP. Mercury shows the token only once at generation ‚ÄĒ rotate from the dashboard if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at install / health_check | 401 ‚ÄĒ token revoked, invalid, or copy-paste truncated | Rotate the token in Mercury Dashboard ‚Üí Settings ‚Üí API Tokens and re-install. |
| `MercuryAuthError(403)` on `send_payment()` only | Token is read-only; reads succeed but writes are forbidden | Mint a Read & Write token in the Mercury dashboard and re-install. |
| `MercuryError: idempotency_key is required` | Caller invoked `send_payment()` with empty `idempotency_key` | Pass `MercuryConnector.new_idempotency_key()` ‚ÄĒ keep the same key across retries of the same logical send. |
| `mercury.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Mercury per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on `get_account()` | Wrong `account_id` or account belongs to a different Mercury org | Verify via `list_accounts()` that the ID exists on this token's org. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'MercuryNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed ‚ÄĒ `MercuryNetworkError = MercuryServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
